questions.question: Which State Launched Its First-Ever Skin Bank Medical College?
questions.explanation: Kerala launched its first-ever, state-run skin bank at the Government Medical College in Thiruvananthapuram, inaugurated around July 15, 2025, to coincide with World Plastic Surgery Day. The facility, aimed at treating burn victims with donated skin, was approved by the Kerala State Organ and Tissue Transplant Organization.

questions.question: Which of the following ruler was Brahmins who gave up their traditional professions and established kingdom in Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Gurjara-Pratihara was a Brahmin who abandoned his traditional priestly duties to take up arms and establish a kingdom in Rajasthan. He is considered the founder of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty, which ruled parts of North-Western India from the 6th century onwards.

questions.question: How Many sub-indices are in Financial Inclusion Index in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Financial Inclusion Index (FI-Index) consists of three key sub-indices: Access (35% weight), Usage (45% weight), and Quality (20% weight). These sub-indices measure the expansion of financial services, actual consumption of financial products, and the quality of services, respectively.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Global Energy Leaders’ Summit (GELS) 2025: • The 2025 summit focused on achieving energy sufficiency while maintaining environmental balance and promoting innovation in the energy sector. • The theme emphasized only renewable energy expansion and excluded conventional energy resources from policy discussions. • The summit aimed to strengthen India’s long-term energy security along with technological advancement
questions.explanation: Statement 1 – Correct The theme clearly highlights three priorities: energy sufficiency, maintaining balance (especially environmental and economic), and innovation. So, this statement matches the intent of the summit. Statement 2 – Incorrect The theme does not restrict discussion only to renewable energy. “Balance” usually means combining conventional, renewable, and emerging energy sources for stability and security. Statement 3 – Correct Energy security, technological progress, and long-term sustainability are central goals of such energy summits, so this interpretation fits well. Hence, the correct answer is A (1 and 3 only)

questions.question: The Export Preparedness Index in India is based on how many pillars?
questions.explanation: The Export Preparedness Index (EPI) released by NITI Aayog in India is based on four main pillars: • Export Infrastructure: Covers physical infrastructure, logistics, and connectivity. • Business Ecosystem: Evaluates the environment for business, including finance, MSME support, and innovation. • Policy & Governance: Assesses state-level export policies and institutional frameworks. • Export Performance: Measures the actual export output, trends, and diversification.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book 'The Illicit Happiness of Other People’?
questions.explanation: The Illicit Happiness of Other People is a 2012 novel by Manu Joseph. The novel explores the interplay of human psychology, existential nihilism and misanthropy, as well as the complexities of adolescence and sexual repression, among other things

questions.question: Which decades are referred as the period of steady population growth?
questions.explanation: The decades 1921–1951 are referred to as the period of steady population growth in India. During this phase, improved healthcare, sanitation, and better transportation reduced mortality rates while birth rates remained high, resulting in a consistent, positive increase in population despite challenges like the Great Depression and World War II. Key details regarding this period (often called Phase II of India's demographic history) include: • Reduced Mortality: Better control of epidemics and improved public health led to a, decrease in the death rate. • High Birth Rate: The crude birth rate remained high, ensuring a steady, upward trend in population growth. • Context: This followed the "stagnant" growth phase of 1901–1921 and preceded the "population explosion" phase of 1951–1981.

questions.question: Which of the following stars is nearest to the sun?
questions.explanation: Proxima Centauri has been the closest star to the sun. It is a small, low-mass star located 4.2465 light years away from the sun in the southern constellation of centaurus. It is a member of the Alpha Centaury star system.

questions.question: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution prohibits employment of children in factories?
questions.explanation: The article 24 of the Indian Constitution accounts for the provisions related to children below 14 years of age. It mentions that no child below the age of fourteen years shall be employed to work in any factory or mine or engaged in any other hazardous employment. But the employment in skillful field and in non-hazardous activities is permitted.

questions.question: When are neap tides produced?
options: When the sun and moon are at right angle to the earth
When the sun lowers the gravitational pull of the moon
When the earth and moon are in a straight line
When the sun partially contracts pull of the earth
questions.explanation: Neap tides are produced in the first and third quarters of the moon. Then the attractions of the Sun and the Moon are at right angles to each other. During the moon's quarter phases, the sun and moon work at right angles, causing the bulges to cancel each other. The result is a smaller difference between high and low tides and is known as a neap tide. Neap tides are weak tides.

questions.question: In which of the following report is/are published under MoSPI? • Periodic Labour Force Survey • Monthly Infrastructure Performance Review Report
questions.explanation: Both the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) and the Monthly Infrastructure Performance Review Report are published by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI). Explanation:- • Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS): This is a primary source of data on employment and unemployment in India. It is conducted by the National Sample Survey Office (NSSO), which operates under MoSPI. As of 2025, MoSPI has revamped the PLFS methodology to provide estimates on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis. • Monthly Infrastructure Performance Review Report: This report is prepared and published by the Infrastructure and Project Monitoring Division (IPMD) of MoSPI. It monitors the performance of 11 key infrastructure sectors (such as Power, Coal, Railways, and Roads) against pre-set targets.

questions.question: Match the Following: Rivers Ancient Name A) Gandak 1.Gomal B) Gomati 2.Askini C) Chenab 3.Vitasta D) Jhelum 4.Sadanira
questions.explanation: The Gandak River originates in the Himalayas of Nepal and flows through Bihar before joining the Ganga. In ancient texts, especially in Vedic and later Puranic literature, it is referred to as Sadanira. The Gomati River (modern Gomti) is a tributary of the Ganga flowing mainly through Uttar Pradesh. In ancient references, the name Gomal appears in Rigvedic texts. The Chenab River is one of the five rivers of Punjab and a major tributary of the Indus. In the Rigveda, it was known as Askini. The Jhelum River originates from the Verinag spring in Kashmir and flows into Pakistan, eventually joining the Chenab. In the Rigveda, it is called Vitasta. In the Rigvedic period, many rivers had different Sanskrit names: • Vitasta → Jhelum • skini → Chenab • Parushni → Ravi • Vipasha → Beas • Shutudri → Sutlej • Sadanira → Gandak

questions.question: Analyze the following statements about Handball rules and court specifications: Assertion (A): The free-throw line in handball is drawn 3 meters outs ide the goal area fine, and it is represented by a broken line. Reason (R): The segments of the free-throw line are 15 cm long, with spaces of 15 cm between them.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. The free-throw line (9-meter line) is a broken line situated 3 meters outside the 6-meter goal area, specifically constructed with 15 cm segments and 15 cm spaces. • Assertion (A): Correct. The 9m line is drawn as a dashed line 3m beyond the 6m goal area boundary. • Reason (R): Correct. The regulation specification for the broken line (dashed) is 15 cm segments with 15 cm spaces, a detail supported by official Handball rule

questions.question: Marble is what type of rock?
questions.explanation: Metamorphic rocks have been modified by heat, pressure and chemical processes, usually while buried deep below Earth's surface. exposure to these extreme conditions has altered the mineralogy, texture and chemical composition of the rocks. There are two basic types of metamorphic rocks. (I) Foliated metamorphic rocks → gneiss, phyllite, schist and slate. (II) Non-foliated metamorphic rocks → hornfels, marble, novaculite quartzite and slate. Marble is metamorphic rock

questions.question: Who among the following has the power to promulgate ordinance?
questions.explanation: Article 123 of the Constitution empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during the recess of Parliament.

questions.question: The Climate Change Performance Index (CCPI) 2O25 evaluates countries based on their performance in:
options: GHG Emissions, Renewable Energy, Energy Use, Climate Policy
Carbon Sequestration, Water Conservation, Biodiversity, Pollution Control
Disaster Prepared ness, Adaptation Strategies, Mitigation Funding, Policy implementation
Green Buildings, Sustainable Transport, Waste Management, Circular Economy
questions.explanation: The Climate Change Performance Index (CCPI) 2025 evaluates 63 countries and the European Union, which together account for over 90% of global greenhouse gas emissions. The index aims to enhance transparency in international climate politics and ranks countries on four key categories based on 14 indicators. The four main pillars of the CCPI 2025 are: • Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions (40%): Evaluates current levels, 5-year trends, and 2°C compatibility. • Renewable Energy (20%): Assesses the share of renewables and growth rates. • Energy Use (20%): Measures energy efficiency and consumption per capita. • Climate Policy (20%): Evaluates national and international climate policies.

questions.question: What is the name of the Official mascot for the Khelo India Para Games 2025?
questions.explanation: The official mascot for the 2nd Khelo India Para Games 2025 is Ujjwala, a sparrow. Unveiled in New Delhi, this mascot represents resilience, determination, and the spirit of the city, highlighting the strength of para-athletes who defy limits. The games are held from March 20–27, 2025, in New Delhi. Key details about the 2025 mascot: • Name: Ujjwala. • Symbolism: Inspired by the house sparrow, representing strength, tenacity, and the indomitable spirit of athletes. • Significance: It highlights the "Champions Beyond Limits" theme of the competition.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of revenue system – person who devised it is correct? • Ryotwari system – Holt Mackenzie • Mahalwari settlement – Alexander Read and Thomas Munro
questions.explanation: Ryotwari System: This system was primarily devised and developed by Alexander Read and Sir Thomas Munro. It was first tried on a small scale by Read in the late 18th century and later implemented across the Madras and Bombay Presidencies by Munro starting around 1820. Mahalwari System: This system was devised by Holt Mackenzie in 1822. It was introduced in the North-Western Provinces of the Bengal Presidency (modern-day Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, and parts of Central India).

questions.question: In which year was Jawaharlal Nehru born?
questions.explanation: Pt. Jawaharlal Nehru was born in Allahabad on 14 November, 1889. He was the first Prime Minister of Independent India. After Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru's death in 1964, to commemorate his birth anniversary and his fondness for children November 14 is celebrated as Children's Day in India

questions.question: What us the force acting on a unit area of a surface called?
questions.explanation: The force acting on a unit area of a surface is called pressure. It is defined as the normal (perpendicular) force applied per unit area.

questions.question: Which of the following is Not a nitrogenous base in DNA?
questions.explanation: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is composed of four specific nitrogenous bases that form its genetic code. While uracil is a nitrogenous base, it is exclusively found in RNA (ribonucleic acid) as a replacement for thymine.

questions.question: The 11th Fundamental Duty of the Indian Constitution is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The fundamental duty to provide opportunities for education to his child or ward between the age of 6-14 years was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act of 2002 as the 11th fundamental duty

questions.question: The "Wood's Despatch" of 1854 recommended several significant changes in the Indian education system. Which of the following was a key recommendation of this dispatch?
options: The immediate vernacularization of all levels of education.
The establishment of a comprehensive system of education from the primary to the university level, with English as the medium of instruction at higher levels.
The complete privatization of the education sector in India.
The exclusive focus on the training of religious leaders and Scholar
questions.explanation: Known as the "Magna Carta of English Education in India," the 1854 Wood's Despatch proposed a structured hierarchy for education. It recommended: • Primary schools should use vernacular languages (mother tongue) to reach the masses. • High schools should use an Anglo-vernacular (combined) approach. • Colleges and Universities should utilize English as the medium of instruction. • The creation of Education Departments in every province and the establishment of universities in Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras.

questions.question: Who was the chairman of the Tax Reforms Committee 1991?
questions.explanation: The chairman of the Tax Reforms Committee (TRC) appointed by the Government of India in August 1991 was Professor Raja J. Chelliah. An eminent economist and former Chief of the Fiscal Analysis Division at the IMF, he was tasked with restructuring both direct and indirect taxes to boost revenue and foster economic growth.

questions.question: In which year did Tipu Sultan become the ruler of Mysore?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan succeeded his father, Hyder Ali, to the throne of the Kingdom of Mysore in December 1782, taking over during the Second Anglo-Mysore War and ruling until his death in 1799

questions.question: The difference between the selling price and cost price of an article is ₹56. If the profit percentage is 4%, what is the selling price of the article?
questions.explanation: Profit/Loss =S⋅P - C⋅P A.T.Q 4% = ₹56 1% = 56/4 = 14 to find S.P 104 × ₹14 = ₹1456

questions.question: A sum of money becomes ₹9100 in 6 years at 5% simple interest per annum. What is the original sum?
If a prism’s height is doubled while the base area remains constant, what happens to the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of prism = base area × height if height is doubled ⇒ base area ×2 height So, Volume become doubled.

questions.question: If 729×√81 = 3x, then, find the value of x.
What is the value of K for which the system of equations 2x + 4y +13 = 0 and Kx + 6y – 9 =0 has no solution?
questions.explanation: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂, if no solution 2/K = 4/6 ⇒ K = 3

questions.question: A square park has an area of 1225 m2. If a footpath 2m wide is constructed around it, what is the total area including the footpath?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_1a32ad63.png) a² = 1225 m² a = 35 m ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_8637c857.png) total area = 39 × 39 = 1521 m²

questions.question: The average of 5 consecutive even numbers is 32. If the next 3 consecutive even numbers are added. Find the new average?
questions.explanation: Let 5 no. are (x-4), (x-2), (x), (x+2), (x+4) x-4 + x-2 + x + x + 2 + x+4 = 32×5 = 160 5x = 160 x = 32 Next 3 consecutive nos =x+6, x+8, x+10 Sum of all 8 nos: =8x+24 put x=32 ⇒8 × 32+24=280 avg. of 8 nos = (sum of all observation)/(Total number of observation) =280/8=35

questions.question: Which of the following schemes will yield a minimum discount? 1) 2 successive discounts of 10% and 5% 2) Single discount of 15% 3) 2 successive discounts of 8% and 7%
If sec3 A =8, then find the value of sin2 A + cosec2 (90-A) + cos2 A where 0°
questions.explanation: sec³A = 8 sec A = 2 sin²A + cosec²(90°-A) + cos²A {sin²A + cos²A = 1} = 1 + sec²A = 1 + (2)² = 5 or sec A = 2 = sec 60° A = 60°

questions.question: A man travels from Bhopal to Mumbai, a distance of 480 km, at a speed of 60 km/h. He then takes a break in Mumbai for 2 hours. After the break, he travels from Mumbai to Goa, a distance of 260km, at a speed of 50 km/h. What is his average speed (approx.) for the entire journey from Bhopal to Goa?
questions.explanation: Bhopal to Mumbai =480km Mumbai to Goa =260km ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_1aa9db83.png)

questions.question: A cylindrical container, 24cm in radius, holds water to a depth of 48cm. If a solid sphere is submerged in the water, the liquid level increases by 12cm. Determine the diameter of the spherical ball?
questions.explanation: Volume of the Liquid level increase = Volume of spherical ball π × 24 × 24 × 12 = 4/3 πr³ r = 12 ∛3 diameter = 2r = 24 ∛3

questions.question: A loan of ₹10,660 is to be paid back in two equal half-yearly instalments. How much is each instalment if the interest is compounded half-yearly at 10% per annum?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_ee193f12.png)

questions.question: P, Q, and R started a business investing ₹1,25,000, ₹1,75,000, ₹2,25,000, respectively. After 6 months P withdrew half of his capital. At the end of the year, what is the ratio of their profits?
In a triangle ABC, ∠B =45°, ∠C =55° , and the bisectors of ∠B and ∠C meet at G. Find ∠BGC.
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_cb4adeba.png) ∠A + ∠B + ∠C = 180° ∠A + 45° + 55° = 180° ∠A = 80° ∠BGC = 90° + (∠A)/2 [G is incenter of △ABC] = 90° + 80°/2 = 130°

questions.question: Which of the following represents positive skewness?
questions.explanation: Positive skewness = Mean > Median > Mode

questions.question: If ratio of mean and median of a certain data is 5:9, then the ratio of its median and mode is.
questions.explanation: mean = 5x Median = 9x mode = 3 median - 2 mean = 3×9x - 2×5x = 17x Median : mode 9 : 17

questions.question: If x + y + z = 15 and xy + yz + zx = 28, find the value of (x - y)² + (y - z)² + (z - x)².
questions.explanation: (x+y+z)² = x² + y² + z² + 2(xy + yz + zx) (15)² = x² + y² + z² + 2(28) x² + y² + z² = 169 ∴ (x-y)² + (y-z)² + (z-x)² = x² + y² + y² + z² + z² + x² - 2xy - 2yz - 2zx = 2(x² + y² + z²) - 2(xy + yz + zx) = 338 - 56 = 282

questions.question: X, Y, and Z can complete a piece of work separately in 20, 30 and 60 days, respectively. In how many days will the work be completed if Y is assisted by both X and Z every third day?

questions.question: A mixture of milk and water contains 90% milk. How much water must be added to 60 liters of mixture to make milk 30%?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_796611d7.png) after adding x litre of water 54L=30% Total mixture =54/30×100=180L ⇒180-60=120L (water added)

questions.question: Find the number of prime factors in the product of (12)⁵ × (20)⁵.
questions.explanation: No. of prime factors ⇒ xa yb zc = a + b + c A.T.Q ⇒ 12⁵ × 20⁵ ⇒ 3⁵ × 2¹⁰ × 5⁵ × 2¹⁰ ⇒ 2²⁰ × 3⁵ × 5⁵ 20 + 5 + 5 = 30

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of playing cards. What is the probability of getting a red color face card?
questions.explanation: Total no. of face cards = 12 (6 red, 6 black) Probability = No. of favorable outcomes / Total no. of outcomes ⇒ 6/52 = 3/26

questions.question: A point 250m away from the tower and the angle of elevation is 60°. Find the height of the tower? (√3=1.732)
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_5fa4edce.png) tan 60° = h/250 = √3 h = 250√3 = 250 × 1.732 = 433 m

questions.question: Two chords AB and CD of a circle intersect at a point G inside the circle. If AG= 8cm, CG= 12cm, and DG= 4cm, what is the length of chord AB?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773334041Math%20Paid%20-%207_html_576b90f3.png) AG × GB = CG × GD 8 × GB = 12 × 4 GB = 6 cm AB = AG + GB = 8 + 6 = 14 cm

questions.question: A student’s marks in English were 40% more than in History. If he scored 98 in History, how many marks did he score in English?
questions.explanation: History marks = 98 English marks = 140/100 (History marks) = (14 × 98)/10 = 137.2

questions.question: The profits of four partners are in the ratio 5:10:13:15. If their profits increase by 40%, 70%, 50%, 100%respectively, what is the new ratio of profits?
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). Replace "does not arrive" with "arrives". 📌 Explanation: Redundancy with negative conjunctions (Until and Unless) - In English grammar, the words "Until" and "Unless" are already negative in meaning: Until means "up to the time that... not." Unless means "if... not." Because these words already carry a negative sense, we cannot use another negative word like "not," "no," or "never" in the clause that follows them. Doing so creates a "double negative" error. Additionally, since the subject "the train" is singular, the verb must be in the singular form (arrives). Correct Sentence: Until the train arrives at the station, we shall wait here.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Even after the heavy financial loss, the startup managed to __________ and eventually became profitable.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - Pull through means to survive or recover from a very difficult situation, a serious illness, or a major setback. In this sentence, it perfectly describes a company successfully navigating a financial crisis to reach profitability. Meanings of other options: Set back: प्रगति रोकना - To delay the progress of someone or something. Example: The bad weather set back the construction of the bridge by several weeks. Give in: हार मान लेना - To finally agree to something that you had been opposing, or to surrender. Example: After a long negotiation, the management finally gave in to the union's demands. Take over: नियंत्रण लेना - To begin to have control of something. Example: The vice president will take over the company's daily operations starting next month.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The detective spent months trying to look for the mysterious disappearance of the lost artifacts, but the trail had gone cold.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "look for" is incorrect in this specific context. While "look for" means to search for a lost object, the sentence describes a detective examining a complex situation or a crime. In English grammar, the phrasal verb "look into" is used when you mean to investigate or examine the facts of a situation. Meanings of Options: Look into: जाँच करना - To investigate or examine the facts of a situation or crime. Look for: खोजना - To search for someone or something that is lost or needed. Look after: देखभाल करना - To take care of someone or something. Look up: खोजना (जानकारी) - To search for information in a book, computer, or list.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: No sooner had he left when it rained.
The jury were divided in their opinion today.
If I was you I would help her.
One should take care of his own health.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) - The jury were divided in their opinion today. 📌 Explanation – This sentence is grammatically correct because it follows the rule of Collective Nouns (Division). When a collective noun like "Jury" indicates that its members are acting individually or are divided in opinion, it must take a plural verb (were) and a plural pronoun (their). Explanation for other wrong options: Option A: Error in Conjunction after "No sooner". The correlative conjunction "No sooner" is always followed by "than" and never by "when." So, replace "when" with "than." Option C: Error in Subjunctive Mood with "If I...". When expressing a hypothetical condition or a situation contrary to fact, the verb "were" is used regardless of the subject (I, He, She, It). So, replace "was" with "were." Option D: Error in Pronoun Agreement with "One". When the sentence starts with the indefinite pronoun "One," the possessive case used later in the sentence must also be "one's." So, replace "his" with "one's."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Despite the loud construction noise outside, the researcher could hardly focus on the complex data analysis required for the report.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (4). 📌 Explanation - The sentence is already grammatically correct. This question tests the distinction between the adjective/adverb "Hard" and the adverb "Hardly." Hard: Means with great effort or solid (e.g., "He works hard"). Hardly: Is a negative adverb meaning "scarcely" or "only just" (e.g., "I can hardly see"). In this context, the sentence correctly expresses that the researcher was scarcely able to focus due to the noise. Furthermore, the modal verb "could" is always followed by the base form of the verb (focus), making the construction "could hardly focus" perfectly accurate.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The diplomat's __________ response to the controversial question successfully avoided a political scandal while still appearing cooperative.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - Equivocal is the most appropriate fit here because it describes language that is open to more than one interpretation or is intentionally ambiguous. In diplomacy, an equivocal response is often used to avoid taking a definitive side on a controversial issue, which aligns with the goal of "avoiding a political scandal." Meanings and explanation of other options: Forthright: प्रत्यक्ष / स्पष्टवादी - (Of a person or their manner) direct and outspoken. Example: The CEO provided a forthright explanation for the company's recent losses. Belligerent: युद्धप्रिय / लड़ाकू - Hostile and aggressive. Example: The player was penalized for his belligerent behavior toward the referee. Dogmatic: कट्टर / सिद्धांतवादी - Inclined to lay down principles as undeniably true, without consideration of others' opinions. Example: He was so dogmatic about his methods that he refused to listen to any alternatives.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (3) – Remove MORE. 📌Error concept: This sentence contains a Double Comparative error. In English grammar, we change an adjective into its comparative form in one of two ways: 1. By adding '-er' to short words (e.g., fast faster). 2. By adding the word 'more' before long words (e.g., beautiful  more beautiful). Using both at the same time ("more faster") is grammatically redundant and incorrect. Since "fast" is a one-syllable word, its comparative form is simply "faster." Correct Sentence: The new software update makes the system run faster than before.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The committee ensured that the selection process was fair and transparent for all candidates.
options: The judge made a fair decision after hearing both sides of the argument.
It is only fair that everyone gets an equal opportunity to participate.
The local agricultural fair attracts thousands of visitors every autumn.
The referee was praised for his fair handling of the intense match.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This is a "Homonym" question. Homonyms are words that have the same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings. In the Question: "Fair" is used as an Adjective which means— treating people equally without favoritism or discrimination (निष्पक्ष / उचित). In Option C: "Fair" is used as a Noun which means— a gathering of people for various entertainment or commercial activities, such as a carnival or exhibition (मेला). This is a completely different meaning and grammatical category, making it the correct homonym choice. WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: All other options (A, B, and D) use FAIR as an Adjective in the sense of being just, equitable, or unbiased, which carries the same core meaning as the highlighted word in the main sentence.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. (A) However, the rapid shift to remote work has also blurred the boundaries between professional and personal life. (B) Digital technology has fundamentally transformed the way modern businesses operate across the globe. (C) This evolution allows employees to collaborate seamlessly regardless of their physical location. (D) Consequently, many organizations are now implementing "right to disconnect" policies to protect employee well-being.
questions.explanation: 👉 The Correct Option is (D): BCAD 📌 Explanation - Step-by-Step Logic: The Opening (Sentence B): This sentence introduces the broad, independent topic ("Digital technology in business"). The Elaboration (Sentence C): It begins with "This evolution...", pointing directly back to the transformation mentioned in Sentence B. It explains a positive result of that technology (collaboration). The Contrast (Sentence A): It starts with the transition word "However," introducing a negative counter-point or challenge (blurred boundaries) to the benefits mentioned in C. The Conclusion (Sentence D): It begins with "Consequently," providing the final result or solution (disconnect policies) to the problem described in Sentence A. FINAL PARAGRAPH: Digital technology has fundamentally transformed the way modern businesses operate across the globe. This evolution allows employees to collaborate seamlessly regardless of their physical location. However, the rapid shift to remote work has also blurred the boundaries between professional and personal life. Consequently, many organizations are now implementing "right to disconnect" policies to protect employee well-being.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Was Vipul being helped by the supervisor when the incident occurred?
options: Was the supervisor helping Vipul when the incident occurred?
Is the supervisor helping Vipul when the incident occurred?
Did the supervisor help Vipul when the incident occurred?
Had the supervisor been helping Vipul when the incident occurred?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - This question tests the ability to maintain tense continuity and interrogative structure while shifting the focus from the object to the subject.  Tense Identification: The passive sentence uses "was being helped," which is the Past Continuous tense in passive form. To maintain accuracy, the active voice must also be in the Past Continuous ("was/were + V-ing").  Subject-Object Swap: In the passive sentence, "Vipul" is the subject and "the supervisor" is the agent. In the active voice, "the supervisor" becomes the subject performing the action.  Interrogative Consistency: Since the original sentence is a question starting with a helping verb, the active version must also start with the appropriate helping verb (Was) followed by the new subject (the supervisor).

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Atul will say, "I am going to the library."
options: Atul will say that he was going to the library.
Atul will say that he is going to the library.
Atul will say that he had been going to the library.
Atul would say that he was going to the library.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation – This question tests the knowledge of the "No-Tense-Change" rule, which applies when the reporting verb is not in the past.  Reporting Verb Check: The reporting verb is "will say" (Simple Future). Unlike when we use "said," a future reporting verb acts as a "shield" that prevents the tense inside the quotes from changing.  Tense Preservation: Since the original speech is in the Present Continuous ("am going"), it must remain in the Present Continuous ("is going") in the indirect form. Option (A) and Option (C) are incorrect because they unnecessarily backshift the tense to the past.  Pronoun Change: While the tense remains the same, pronouns must still change to reflect the new speaker. "I" correctly changes to "he" to refer back to Atul.  Reporting Verb Consistency: The reporting verb "will say" must remain "will say."

questions.question: Researchers from Nagaland University ___ (1) ___ uncovered the climate adaptability of a wild-seeded banana species native to the Eastern Himalayas, underlining its potential role in food security and sustainable agricultural development. Their extensive study on the genetic ___ (2) ___ of Musa sikkimensis, also called the 'Darjeeling banana' or 'Sikkim banana', has been published in the latest issue of Flora and Fauna, a peer-reviewed international journal. The authors of the study are K.R. Singh, S.Walling, and Animesh Sarkar of the university's Department of Horticulture. According to the study, the Sikkim banana serves as a vital genetic ___ (3) ___ for disease resistance, stress tolerance, and climate adaptability traits considered essential as climate change threatens global banana production. ___ (4) ___ not widely cultivated as an edible fruit, the species plays a role in strengthening crop resilience and ensuring sustainable production. The research team found that local banana germplasm exhibits strong adaptive potential across diverse ___ (5) ___ conditions. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B): have. 📌 Explanation: The subject of the sentence is "Researchers," which is a plural noun. To form the present perfect tense ("...uncovered"), we must use the plural auxiliary verb "have." Why other options are wrong: 1. "Has" is singular and does not agree with the plural subject "Researchers." 2. "Was" and "Is" are used for passive voice or continuous tenses, but here we need the active present perfect to show a completed action with current relevance. Furthermore, "is" is singular.

questions.question: Researchers from Nagaland University ___ (1) ___ uncovered the climate adaptability of a wild-seeded banana species native to the Eastern Himalayas, underlining its potential role in food security and sustainable agricultural development. Their extensive study on the genetic ___ (2) ___ of Musa sikkimensis, also called the 'Darjeeling banana' or 'Sikkim banana', has been published in the latest issue of Flora and Fauna, a peer-reviewed international journal. The authors of the study are K.R. Singh, S.Walling, and Animesh Sarkar of the university's Department of Horticulture. According to the study, the Sikkim banana serves as a vital genetic ___ (3) ___ for disease resistance, stress tolerance, and climate adaptability traits considered essential as climate change threatens global banana production. ___ (4) ___ not widely cultivated as an edible fruit, the species plays a role in strengthening crop resilience and ensuring sustainable production. The research team found that local banana germplasm exhibits strong adaptive potential across diverse ___ (5) ___ conditions. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C): diversity. 📌 Explanation: In biological sciences, the term "genetic diversity" is a specific technical phrase used to describe the total range of genetic characteristics within a species. It is the most appropriate word for a peer-reviewed journal context. Why other options are wrong: 1."Variety" and "Types" are too general and informal for a research study; "variety" usually refers to a specific subspecies or a collection of different things rather than the internal genetic range. 2."Differences" is a broad term that lacks the scientific precision required to describe a study's focus on the entire genetic pool of the Musa sikkimensis.

questions.question: Researchers from Nagaland University ___ (1) ___ uncovered the climate adaptability of a wild-seeded banana species native to the Eastern Himalayas, underlining its potential role in food security and sustainable agricultural development. Their extensive study on the genetic ___ (2) ___ of Musa sikkimensis, also called the 'Darjeeling banana' or 'Sikkim banana', has been published in the latest issue of Flora and Fauna, a peer-reviewed international journal. The authors of the study are K.R. Singh, S.Walling, and Animesh Sarkar of the university's Department of Horticulture. According to the study, the Sikkim banana serves as a vital genetic ___ (3) ___ for disease resistance, stress tolerance, and climate adaptability traits considered essential as climate change threatens global banana production. ___ (4) ___ not widely cultivated as an edible fruit, the species plays a role in strengthening crop resilience and ensuring sustainable production. The research team found that local banana germplasm exhibits strong adaptive potential across diverse ___ (5) ___ conditions. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B): reservoir. 📌 Explanation: In genetics and conservation biology, a "reservoir" refers to a population or species that contains and preserves a large supply of diverse genetic traits. It implies a "source" that can be tapped into for future breeding or survival, which fits the academic tone of the research study. Why other options are wrong: 1."Tank" and "Container" are too literal and refer to physical objects for storage; they are not used metaphorically in scientific literature to describe genetic traits. 2."Basement" is completely irrelevant to the context of biology and suggests a physical location rather than a collection of assets.

questions.question: Researchers from Nagaland University ___ (1) ___ uncovered the climate adaptability of a wild-seeded banana species native to the Eastern Himalayas, underlining its potential role in food security and sustainable agricultural development. Their extensive study on the genetic ___ (2) ___ of Musa sikkimensis, also called the 'Darjeeling banana' or 'Sikkim banana', has been published in the latest issue of Flora and Fauna, a peer-reviewed international journal. The authors of the study are K.R. Singh, S.Walling, and Animesh Sarkar of the university's Department of Horticulture. According to the study, the Sikkim banana serves as a vital genetic ___ (3) ___ for disease resistance, stress tolerance, and climate adaptability traits considered essential as climate change threatens global banana production. ___ (4) ___ not widely cultivated as an edible fruit, the species plays a role in strengthening crop resilience and ensuring sustainable production. The research team found that local banana germplasm exhibits strong adaptive potential across diverse ___ (5) ___ conditions. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C): Although 📌 Explanation - "Although" is a conjunction used to introduce a contrast. Here, it contrasts the fact that the banana isn't popular for eating with the fact that it is extremely important for scientific research and crop strength. Why other options are wrong: (a) Because: This is a causal connector. If you use "Because," you are saying the banana is important specifically because people don't eat it. This is logically incorrect; its importance comes from its genetics, not from its lack of popularity as a snack. (b) Despite: This is a preposition, not a conjunction. While "Despite" expresses the correct "contrast" logic, it cannot be followed directly by a clause or a participle phrase in this way.  Grammar Check: To use "Despite," the sentence would need to be: "Despite not being widely cultivated..." or "Despite the fact that it is not widely cultivated..." (d) Since: Similar to "Because," "Since" usually indicates a reason (e.g., "Since it was raining, I stayed home"). Using it here would suggest that the lack of cultivation is the reason for its crucial role, which makes no sense in the context of the research.

questions.question: Researchers from Nagaland University ___ (1) ___ uncovered the climate adaptability of a wild-seeded banana species native to the Eastern Himalayas, underlining its potential role in food security and sustainable agricultural development. Their extensive study on the genetic ___ (2) ___ of Musa sikkimensis, also called the 'Darjeeling banana' or 'Sikkim banana', has been published in the latest issue of Flora and Fauna, a peer-reviewed international journal. The authors of the study are K.R. Singh, S.Walling, and Animesh Sarkar of the university's Department of Horticulture. According to the study, the Sikkim banana serves as a vital genetic ___ (3) ___ for disease resistance, stress tolerance, and climate adaptability traits considered essential as climate change threatens global banana production. ___ (4) ___ not widely cultivated as an edible fruit, the species plays a role in strengthening crop resilience and ensuring sustainable production. The research team found that local banana germplasm exhibits strong adaptive potential across diverse ___ (5) ___ conditions. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D): environmental 📌 Explanation - In this sentence, we need an adjective to modify the noun "conditions." "Environmental" is the adjective form of the word "environment" and correctly describes the type of conditions being discussed. Why other options are wrong: 1. "Environment" is a noun. While nouns can sometimes act as modifiers, "environmental" is the standard and grammatically correct adjective for this context. 2. "Environmentally" is an adverb. Adverbs modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs, but they cannot directly modify a noun like "conditions." 3. "Environmenting" is not a standard English word.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Bestow Let’s see the meaning of all words: Puissant - पराक्रमी - having great power or influence Example: The puissant king ruled over a vast empire Feigned - नकली - pretended or simulated Example: She feigned interest in the boring lecture Bestow - बख्शना - to give or grant something Example: The king bestowed a title on his loyal servant Coarseness - खुरदरापन - the quality of being rough or crude Example: The coarseness of the fabric made it uncomfortable to wear

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The company ripped off customers with their exorbitant prices.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Duped. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Ripped - फाड़ दिया - torn or pulled apart Example: She ripped the paper into pieces and threw it away Duped - धोखा दिया - deceived or cheated Example: The customers were duped by the fake sale offers Options: Tremor - झटका - a shaking or vibrating movement Example: The earthquake caused a tremor in the building Patron - संरक्षक - a customer or supporter Example: The restaurant's patrons enjoyed the live music Regain - वापस पाना - to get something back Example: She worked hard to regain her confidence

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Ineluctable
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Escapable Let’s see the meaning of all words: Ineluctable - अपरिहार्य - unavoidable or inevitable Example: The ineluctable fate awaited him. Genteel - विनम्र - refined or polite Example: The genteel lady was admired for her kindness. Escapable - बच निकलने योग्य - able to be avoided Example: The situation was difficult but escapable with careful planning. Sloth - आलस्य - laziness Example: His sloth led to poor performance at work. Sterility - बंजरता - the state of being unable to produce Example: The land's sterility made it hard to grow crops.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Made light of
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Treated it lightly Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Made light of - कम महत्व दिया - treated something as unimportant or insignificant Example: He made light of his struggles with depression, saying everyone goes through tough times.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Shriek
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Squeal Let’s see the meaning of all words: Shriek - चिल्लाना - a loud, high-pitched cry or scream Example: She let out a shriek when she saw the spider. Deride - उपहास करना - to mock or ridicule Example: He derided her efforts to cook. Grimace - मुँह बनाना - to make a face expressing pain or disgust Example: She grimaced at the bitter taste. Squeal - किकियाना - a high-pitched cry or scream Example: The child squealed with delight. Foams - झाग - a mass of bubbles Example: The soap foams up when you mix it with water.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The old bridge's parapet was crumbling, making it unsafe to walk on.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Void Let’s see the meaning of all words: Parapet - परपेट - a low wall or barrier at the edge of a bridge Example: She leaned against the parapet, gazing out at the city skyline. Haywire - अव्यवस्थित - out of control or chaotic Example: The plan went haywire after the unexpected storm. Attest - प्रमाणित करना - to confirm or verify Example: The witness attested to the defendant's alibi. Void - शून्य - an empty space or gap Example: The contract was declared void due to technical issues. Coax - मनाना - to persuade gently Example: She coaxed the child to eat the vegetables.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Characterized by tolerance and mercy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Lenient Let’s see the meaning of all words: Narrator - कथावाचक - a person who tells a story Example: The narrator of the documentary did a great job. Lenient - सहिष्णु - showing tolerance and mercy Example: The judge was lenient with the first-time offender. Ovine - भेड़ से संबंधित - relating to sheep Example: The ovine animals were grazing in the field. Parboil - आंशिक रूप से उबालना - to boil partially Example: Parboil the potatoes before roasting them.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. After years of corruption scandals, the public has developed a jaundiced eye towards politicians.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - A prejudiced view Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Jaundiced eye - पक्षपाती दृष्टि - a biased or skeptical view, often due to past experiences or prejudices Example: The media is viewing the government's policies with a jaundiced eye.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A person of no influence
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Nonentity Let’s see the meaning of all words: Tacky - चिपचिपा - sticky or cheap and tasteless Example: The decor was tacky and outdated. Pallid - पीला - pale or lacking color Example: The patient's pallid face worried the doctor Nonentity - महत्वहीन व्यक्ति - a person of no importance or influence Example: In the corporate world, he's a complete nonentity. Outcast - बहिष्कृत - a person rejected or excluded from society Example: She felt like an outcast at the party.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs which will replace the * symbol to make the given equation correct? 1295*37*86*15*3*76

A number and word arrangement machine when given an input of numbers and words, rearranges them following a particular rule. The following is an illustration of input and arrangement. Step 5 is the last step of the machine. Input: ssc 62 cgl 78 ibps 57 cpo 25 jee 15 Step 1: 15 62 cgl 78 ibps 57 cpo 25 jee ssc Step 2: 15 25 62 cgl 78 ibps 57 cpo ssc jee Step 3: 15 25 57 62 78 ibps cpo ssc jee cgl Step 4: 15 25 57 62 78 ibps ssc jee cgl cpo Step 5: 15 25 57 62 78 ssc jee cgl cpo ibps As per rule followed in the above steps, determine the number of steps required to find the output for the input given below: Input: have 24 you 78 taken 46 your 55 lunch 32
questions.explanation: Input: have 24 you 78 taken 46 your 55 lunch 32 Step 1: 24 you 78 taken 46 your 55 lunch 32 have Step 2: 24 32 you 78 taken 46 your 55 have lunch Step 3: 24 32 46 78 taken your 55 have lunch you Step 4: 24 32 46 55 78 taken have lunch you your Step 5 (final): 24 32 46 55 78 have lunch you your taken So, Option B is correct.

questions.question: The present age of Sandeep is 5 years more than his friend Rupa. 4 years hence, the age of Sandeep will be two times that of his sister Garima. If the present age of Garima is 16 years, then what is the present age of Rupa?
questions.explanation: Garima present age → 16 4 years hence → 16+4 = 20 Sandeep age 4 years hence → 20×2=40 years Sandeep present age = 40-4 = 36 years Rupa present age = 36-5 = 31 years Option C is correct.

questions.question: What day of the week was 19th Jan 1978?
questions.explanation: Odd days for the century year 1900 = 1 (Standard) 1900-1977 →77+19 leap year → 96/7⇒5 19 Jan 1978 :- 19/7→ 5 Total extra days :- 5+5+1 = 11 11/7→4⇒Thursday Option B is correct

questions.question: In a row Soumya is 17th from the front end and 12th from the back end. How many people are sitting in the row?
questions.explanation: Total Number of People in the row = 17+12-1 = 17+11= 28 people

Employees often arrive late to meetings.
options: Both assumption 1 and assumption 2 are implicit
